parts.title: An Ordinary Beginning
parts.text: Nikhil grew up in an **ordinary** town where nothing remarkable seemed to happen. She was a quiet child with one enormous **ambition**: to write a book that children everywhere would read. She spent a **portion** of every evening writing in her notebook, building her story sentence by sentence.

parts.title: Finding Her Voice
parts.text: Nikhil began to **volunteer** at the school library, helping younger students choose books and practise reading aloud. One afternoon she noticed a **link** between the stories that children loved most — they all had a character who refused to give up. She wanted her book to reach that same high **ridge** of feeling, the place where a story lifts you out of your everyday life. Meera, the librarian, told her that the best stories came from the deepest part of a writer's own experience.

parts.title: The Big Break
parts.text: Nikhil sent her manuscript to twelve publishers. The story came from a small **region** of her imagination she had never shown anyone before. Finally, one publisher agreed to **publish** it. The **media** arrived at her school the day the book launched — cameras, reporters, and microphones filling the front hall. Lakshmi, her teacher, stood beside her like a **rudder**, steady and guiding, as Nikhil answered question after question.

parts.title: A Greener Future
parts.text: The book sold quickly, and the audience at the launch was so moved that they demanded an **encore** — Nikhil read a second chapter aloud to a room that had gone completely still. She was **diminutive** in size but enormous in presence, standing at the podium with her voice clear and strong. Meera told the newspapers, "Nikhil showed us that one person with a good idea and the courage to share it can change everything." The book went on to be read in schools across the country.

definitions.meaning1: Ways of communicating information to many people, such as television, newspapers, and the internet. Example: The school principal spoke to the media about the students' award-winning science project.
definitions.studentChallenge: News travels fast because ___ like television and the internet can share stories with millions of people.

definitions.meaning1: An extra performance given at the end of a concert because the audience wants more. Example: The crowd clapped and cheered until the band came back for an encore.
definitions.studentChallenge: The singer performed two songs as an ___ after the audience kept cheering for more.

definitions.meaning1: A connection between two things. Example: Scientists found a link between eating healthy food and feeling more energetic.
definitions.meaning2: One ring or loop of a chain. Example: The chain was only as strong as its weakest link.
definitions.studentChallenge: The detective discovered a ___ between the two clues that helped solve the mystery.

definitions.meaning1: A person who does work without being paid. Example: The volunteer helped serve food at the shelter.
definitions.studentChallenge: She decided to ___ at the animal rescue center.

definitions.meaning1: A long, narrow raised area along the top of something, such as a mountain or a roof. Example: The hikers walked along the ridge of the hill and enjoyed views of both valleys below.
definitions.studentChallenge: From the ___ of the mountain, we could see the sparkling ocean in the distance.

definitions.meaning1: A large area of land, often with particular features or characteristics. Example: The northern region of the country is covered in thick forests and tall mountains.
definitions.studentChallenge: The desert ___ receives very little rainfall and has extremely hot temperatures during the day.

definitions.meaning1: A flat piece attached to the back of a boat or aeroplane that is used to steer it. Example: The captain turned the rudder to guide the ship safely into the harbour.
definitions.studentChallenge: Without a working ___, the sailboat drifted aimlessly across the lake.

definitions.meaning1: Extremely small; tiny. Example: The diminutive kitten fit in the palm of her hand.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ flowers grew between the cracks in the sidewalk.

definitions.meaning1: A part or share of something, especially an amount of food served to one person. Example: Each child received an equal portion of the birthday cake.
definitions.studentChallenge: She saved a ___ of her pocket money each week to buy a new book.

definitions.meaning1: A strong desire to achieve something. Example: Her ambition is to become an astronaut.
definitions.studentChallenge: With hard work and ___, you can achieve your goals.

definitions.meaning1: To prepare and print a book, newspaper, or other written work so people can read it. Example: The author was thrilled when a company agreed to publish her first children's book.
definitions.studentChallenge: The school decided to ___ a magazine filled with stories and poems written by the students.

definitions.meaning1: Normal; not special or different. Example: It seemed like an ordinary day until the surprise happened.
definitions.studentChallenge: She turned an ___ rock into a work of art.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The school principal spoke to reporters and camera crews — all the ___ — about the students' science award.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: News travels fast today because ___ like television and the internet can share stories with millions instantly.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The crowd clapped and cheered so loudly that the band came back out for an extra performance — an ___.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The singer performed two bonus songs as an ___ after the audience refused to stop cheering for more.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Scientists found a clear ___ — a direct connection — between eating healthy food and having more energy.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The detective discovered a ___ between the two clues that finally helped her crack the mystery wide open.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ gave up her Saturday to serve hot meals at the shelter, working without pay from dawn to dusk.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: She decided to ___ at the animal rescue center, choosing to give her free time to help animals in need.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The hikers walked in single file along the narrow ___ at the top of the hill, with valleys dropping away on both sides.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: From the ___ of the mountain, they could see the glittering ocean stretching out far below.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The northern ___ of the country is covered in thick, dark forests and tall, snow-capped mountains.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The desert ___ receives very little rainfall and bakes under extremely hot temperatures every day.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The captain turned the ___ sharply to steer the ship away from the rocks and safely into the harbour.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Without a working ___, the small sailboat drifted helplessly and aimlessly in circles across the lake.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ kitten — so tiny it could fit in the palm of a hand — mewed softly inside the cardboard box.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The ___ flowers pushing up between the sidewalk cracks were so small you could easily miss them.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Each child received an equal ___ of the birthday cake — every slice cut to exactly the same size.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: She saved a small ___ of her pocket money each week, setting it aside to buy a new book at month's end.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Her ___ — her powerful, burning desire — was to become the first astronaut from her small hometown.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: With hard work and ___, you can turn even the most distant dream into something real.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The author was overjoyed when a well-known company agreed to ___ her very first children's book.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The school planned to ___ a student magazine filled with poems, stories, and artwork every term.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: It seemed like an ___ Tuesday — nothing unusual at all — until the surprise party guests jumped out.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: She took an ___ grey rock and painted it so beautifully that it looked like a tiny work of art.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage: Twelve-year-old Priya had one great _(1)_ — a burning desire to write a book that would be read by children all over the world. She did not come from a famous family or a wealthy _(2)_ of the country; she grew up in a perfectly _(3)_ town where nothing unusual ever seemed to happen. Every day after school, she would _(4)_ at the community library, helping shelve books and read stories to younger children without being paid a single coin. She began writing her story in a _(5)_ notebook so small it fit inside her coat pocket. The plot had a strong _(6)_ — a clear connection — between a lonely girl and a magical forest that seemed to mirror the girl's own feelings. Scientists in the book studied the mountain _(7)_, the long narrow peak that ran above the valley, and discovered something astonishing hidden beneath it. One day, reporters from every _(8)_ — newspapers, television channels, and websites — came to interview Priya's teacher about the school's writing programme. Her teacher mentioned Priya's story, and suddenly the whole country was listening. A publisher offered to _(9)_ the book immediately, agreeing to print and share it with readers everywhere. At the book launch, the crowd cheered so loudly that Priya had to give an _(10)_ — reading an extra chapter aloud because the audience simply would not let her stop. She used the _(11)_ — the steering piece — of her imagination to guide each new chapter in an exciting direction. She set aside a _(12)_ of every day for writing, protecting that quiet hour like a treasure, and it was that daily habit that turned her dream into a reality.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
